simpleExplanation.title: Business and the international economy — explained simply
simpleExplanation.summary: Think of business and the international economy as one building block in your Paper 2 (Case Study) toolkit. Examiners rarely ask for memorised paragraphs — they want you to apply the idea in a structured answer.
simpleExplanation.analogy: If the full lesson feels dense, focus on one past-paper question: read the mark scheme after attempting it — that shows what Cambridge actually rewards.

faq.q: Is this Business Studies Business and the international economy lesson free?
faq.a: Yes — every topic in our 7115 course is free. MarkScheme offers free past-paper marking so you can turn notes into marks.
Scroll to the past-paper section on this page — we surface real Cambridge questions tagged to 6.3 from our mark-scheme library.

sections.content: Welcome to your **free Business Studies** lesson on **Business and the international economy** (6.3). This is syllabus point 6.3 on **Paper 2 (Case Study)** — structured like a premium revision course, with past-paper practice built in.

items: Master the vocabulary examiners use in mark schemes for Business and the international economy
Know which paper (P2) tests this topic most often
Practise at least one official past-paper question after reading
Use “Explain simpler” below if the main lesson feels too advanced

sections.content: Cambridge 7115 rewards answers tied to the case business — name the firm, quote stimulus data, and balance advantages against disadvantages. On Paper 2 (Case Study), Business and the international economy often appears as part of a longer structured or case question.
